name: Poppy Seed Pasta
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Chopped fresh or dried herb, crushed,
recipeInstructions:
If using self-rising flour, omit the 1/2 teaspoon salt.
1. Mix flour, poppy seeds, herb and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a large bowl.
2. Make a well in the center of the flour mixture.
3. Add eggs, 1/4 cup water, and oil to the well and mix thoroughly.
- If the dough is too dry, add a few drops of water.
- If the dough is too sticky, add a small amount of flour.
4. Gather the dough into a ball and knead on a floured surface for about 5 minutes
until smooth and elastic.
5. Divide the dough into 4 equal parts.
6. Roll each part out into a paper-thin rectangle on a generously floured surface,
keeping the remaining dough covered.
7. Loosely fold the rectangle lengthwise into thirds.
8. If using a pasta machine, pass the dough through until it is 1/16-inch thick.
9. Bring 4-1/2 quarts of water to a boil in a large pot.
10. Stir in 1/4 teaspoon salt and add the pasta.
11. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes or until almost tender, then drain.
8 SERVINGS (ABOUT 3/4 CUP EACH); 140 CALORIES PER SERVING.
